interactions.summary: Jiang tracks baby-boomer power from the Great Society through Reagan, the unipolar 1990s, Covid, Gaza campus protests, and predicts boomers will make America more authoritarian before they die.
Dave accepts Jiang's point and recalls how powerfully American culture dominated the world around the unipolar moment, using Metallica in Moscow as an example.
The call briefly drops and resumes, with Sneako joking that the CCP did not get Jiang.

claims.claim: Jiang says baby boomers enjoyed a uniquely favorable life course: Great Society social supports, Reagan-era wealth opportunities, and unipolar American privilege.
Jiang says Covid lockdowns benefited boomers with assets and health-risk concerns while damaging young people's mental health, schooling, and economic prospects.
Jiang predicts boomers will make America more authoritarian before they die so they can live peacefully in gated communities and continue extracting from the republic.
Dave says American culture had extraordinary global goodwill around the Soviet collapse, symbolized by heavy-metal enthusiasm in Moscow.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang calls boomers parasites on the American Republic who will harden the state before they die.
signature_moments.source_phrase: continue to be parasites on the American Republic
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the sharpest generational accusation in the source and becomes a prediction about political form.

signature_moments.moment: Dave's image of Metallica in Moscow carries the vanished appeal of American culture.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives the decline story a cultural memory rather than only balance sheets and wars.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's prediction that aging boomers will preserve comfort by making America more authoritarian before leaving power.
The period after the Soviet collapse when U.S. power and culture appeared globally dominant.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang's generational timeline runs Great Society to Reagan to the 1990s unipolar moment to Trump 2016, Covid, October 7, and campus protest crackdowns.
